mechanism_narrative: A sustained upward drift in FEDFUNDS from near-zero levels signals the start of a monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates on future equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as the trend persists.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% in Dec 2021 with FOMC signaling 2022 hikes
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month change and 20d forward SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive FEDFUNDS trends coincide with positive forward returns more than 55% of the time.

mechanism_narrative: When CPIAUCSL year-over-year inflation crosses above 6%, market participants price in accelerated Fed tightening, compressing valuation multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent quarter until inflation visibly decelerates.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL vintage_value=280.192 revised_value=280.845 in Dec 2021, well above prior-year trend implying YoY inflation near 7%
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following CPI-YoY>6% triggers are positive in more than 55% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong PAYEMS job growth and falling UNRATE indicate a labor market approaching full employment, raising wage-inflation risk that pressures the Fed toward faster tightening; this joint signal predicts negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days beyond what either series predicts alone.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month annualized growth > 2% while UNRATE falls > 0.2pp over same period
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% and PAYEMS rising to 149816 (revised) in Dec 2021 indicating tightening labor market
failure_condition: The interaction term (PAYEMS growth x UNRATE decline) shows no incremental predictive power over univariate models (interaction coefficient not significant at p<0.10) across rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime, elevated RSAFS retail sales levels reflect robust consumer demand momentum that supports near-term corporate revenue beats, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days following strong monthly retail sales prints.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS revised_value=619938 million in Dec 2021 remaining near record highs
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns after RSAFS month-over-month increases are not significantly different from unconditional 5-day return distribution (t-test p > 0.10) across the sample.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in INDPRO indicates expanding industrial output and corporate earnings capacity, which supports higher equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter as long as the trend continues uninterrupted.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO revised_value=100.5726 in Dec 2021, up from prior months indicating continued industrial expansion
failure_condition: Rank IC between INDPRO 6-month trend and 60d forward SPX returns is below 0.03 over any rolling 3-year window, or negative INDPRO trends do not coincide with below-median forward returns.

mechanism_narrative: The first crossing of FEDFUNDS above its multi-year near-zero floor (a policy liftoff signal) triggers an immediate repricing of short-duration growth equities, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets front-run the tightening cycle.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 0.25% for the first time after a period below that level for 12+ months
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS at 0.08% in Dec 2021 with market pricing March 2022 liftoff
failure_condition: 5-day forward SPX returns around the liftoff crossing event are positive in more than 55% of historical liftoff episodes, or effect is not statistically distinguishable from zero (p>0.10).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in HOUST housing starts occurring jointly with a rising FEDFUNDS rate signals that higher financing costs are already suppressing rate-sensitive demand, foreshadowing broader economic slowdown and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days beyond either series alone.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls > 5% over 3 months while FEDFUNDS rises over the same period
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST revised_value=1705 thousand units in Nov 2021 near cycle highs, at risk from anticipated 2022 rate hikes
regime_hint: monetary tightening cycle with rate-sensitive demand exposure
failure_condition: Interaction coefficient of HOUST decline and FEDFUNDS rise on 20d forward returns is not significant at p<0.10, or joint-trigger episodes produce positive forward returns more often than negative ones.

mechanism_narrative: In a regime where UNRATE has been falling steadily for over a year and approaches historical full-employment lows, a subsequent reversal where UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points signals an emerging cyclical downturn (Sahm-rule-like dynamic), producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following quarter.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises > 0.3pp from its trailing 12-month low
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE at 3.9% in Dec 2021, near multi-year lows after steady post-pandemic decline
failure_condition: 60-day forward SPX returns following UNRATE upturns of >0.3pp from cycle lows are positive in more than 50% of historical instances, or Rank IC < 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.
